meaning: n.讼棍,奸诈之徒(尤指政客)
examples: Lafferty's longtime business partner, Allan Aitken, told me that he believed that "Biro was either a shyster or a con man, and had found in Lafferty an easy mark."
When your soul is on the line, distinguishing between a marketing guru and a shyster with a pyramid involves much higher stakes.
